author_headline: SDE Intern @ Amazon | Codeforces Candidate Master (Max 1938) | LeetCode Guardian (Max 2240) | CodeChef 4⭐ (Max 1961) | B.Tech CSE @ IIITDM Jabalpur
content:
From Newbie to Candidate Master on Codeforces 💜

When I initially started competitive programming, I had never even imagined I would become a Candidate Master.

Back then, solving a single problem felt like an achievement, and the idea of seeing a purple rating beside my username seemed impossible. I was just another beginner trying to understand algorithms and hoping to improve one contest at a time.

Today, I'm incredibly happy to share that I've reached Candidate Master (1938) on Codeforces.

This journey wasn't easy. There were countless wrong submissions, frustrating rating drops, contests where nothing went my way, and moments when I questioned myself. But every setback taught me something, and every small improvement kept me moving forward.

The biggest lesson I've learned is that consistency beats everything, and mindset is everything. If you truly believe you can achieve something and are willing to put in the work every single day, you'll eventually get there.

A special thank you to my buddies Aayush Tewary and Suseel Kumar Giddaluri for being there throughout this journey. From discussing problems and upsolving contests to celebrating milestones and pushing through setbacks together, I couldn't have asked for better companions.

I'm also grateful to everyone who has supported me along the way—friends, seniors, mentors, and the competitive programming community. Every discussion and every bit of encouragement played a part in this milestone.
This is a milestone I'm proud of, but it's just another step in the journey. There's still so much to learn, so many harder problems to solve, and many more goals to chase.

One more milestone achieved, many more to chase. On to the next one!

#Codeforces #CompetitiveProgramming #CandidateMaster #Algorithms #DataStructures #ProblemSolving #Coding #Consistency #GrowthMindset #Learning #IIITDMJabalpur #SoftwareDeveloper

author_name: Homi Bhabha Centre for Science Education (HBCSE), TIFR
content:
Another Golden Sweep by India as our Chemistry team bags 4 Gold medals securing Rank #1 (jointly with China, Vietnam, and Individual Participant (Russia)) at the 58th International Chemistry Olympiad (@icho.official).

There were 363 participants from 93 countries competing in IChO 2026 held in Tashkent, Uzbekistan from July 10-19, 2026. This was India's 27th participation in the IChO and best performance till date.

Heartiest Congratulations to the entire team!!

1. Debadatta Priyadarshi 🥇Gold, Bhubaneswar, Odisha
2. Harshit Singla 🥇Gold, Mandi Gobindgarh, Punjab
3. Kabeer Chhillar 🥇 Gold, Delhi
4. Sandeep Kuchi 🥇 Gold, Hyderabad, Telangana

The team was accompanied by Head Mentor Prof. Subhajit Bandyopadhyay (IISER Kolkata), Mentor Dr. Indrani Das Sen (HBCSE), and Scientific Observers Dr. Anubendu Adhikary (IIT Kharagpur) and Dr. Jayasree Gopalakrishnan (NES Ratnam College, Mumbai).

We thank the National Steering Committee on Science Olympiads, teacher organisations, and the extended family of resource persons with the Chemistry Olympiad Cell for their strong unwavering support through all stages of the National program. We acknowledge the generous funding from DAE, DST, and MoE, GoI.

#IChO_2026, #olympiads #chemistry

author_headline: SWE @ Microsoft | 75K+ LinkedIn | Tech, AI and Marketing | Codeforces
content:
Most people don't quit because the work is hard.

They quit because complaining feels easier.

It's easy to blame your college, your manager, the interview process, or luck.
Complaining gives temporary relief, but it never changes your situation.

The day you replace complaints with action, everything starts to change.

Read one chapter.
Solve one coding problem.
Apply for one opportunity.
Ask one question.
Improve by just 1% today.

Small actions may not feel exciting, but they compound over time.

Complaining keeps you where you are.
Consistency takes you where you want to be.
